body_txt: Most Mooks exist in video games for one reason and one reason only: to make your life hard (and your death easy) . They touch you , they spit stuff at you , they push you around... However, sometimes the designers mix things up at bit, and you'll run into a Helpful Mook. While they look like mooks, walk like mooks, and talk like mooks—they even generally show up on your Enemy Scan as a baddie—these guys aren't as dangerous as their companions. They generally come in two flavors:  May be used as a form of Fairy Battle . Supertrope of Frozen Foe Platform , as the mook has to be coerced into being helpful as an Improvised Platform . Often, but not always, overlaps with Harmless Enemy . Compare Savage Setpiece .
